channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hezbollah announces funeral for slain longtime leader Hassan Nasrallah set for Feb 23
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese Hezbollah group Sunday announced the funeral of its longtime leader will take place on Feb. 23, months after he was assassinated in a series of Israeli airstrikes in a southern Beirut suburb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's trade war among allies triggers retaliation from Canada and Mexico
item.description: Canada and Mexico ordered retaliatory tariffs on American goods in response to sweeping tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies And Maria Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Americans could feel 'some pain' from his new tariffs that are triggering a trade war
item.description: President Donald Trump says Americans could feel “some pain” from the emerging trade war triggered by his tariffs against Canada, Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer say he will seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after hitting America’s biggest trading partners with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: London, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: China’s Ministry of Commerce said it would file a legal case against the United States at the World Trade Organization (WTO), in response to President Trump’s decision Saturday to impose 10 percent tariffs on Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: France braces for a political showdown as the new prime minister seeks to force a budget through
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Political uncertainty is again looming in France after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou warned that he would use special executive powers this week to get his budget approved without a vote by lawmakers. The move is expected to trigger a motion of no confidence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu departs for US to meet with Trump, hoping to strengthen ties with Washington
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu left for the U.S. on Sunday to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, looking to strengthen ties with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade blame over an attack on a boarding school in Russia’s Kursk region
item.description: Competing claims have emerged over a deadly attack on a boarding school in Sudzha, a city in Russia’s Kursk region that has been under Ukrainian control for five months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu heads to US to discuss 'victory over Hamas' with Trump
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he will discuss “victory over Hamas,” countering Iran and expanding diplomatic relations with Arab countries in his meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: King Charles says 'our hearts' are with United States after 'devastating loss of life' in DC plane crash
item.description: King Charles III said Saturday he was "shocked and saddened" about the deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport Wednesday, saying his thoughts were with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sick and wounded children begin crossing into Egypt as Rafah crossing opens after several months
item.description: RAFAH CROSSING, Egypt (AP) — A group of 50 sick and wounded Palestinian children began entering Egypt for treatment through Gaza’s Rafah crossing on Saturday, the first opening of the border since Israel captured it nearly nine months ago.
item.dc:creator: AHMED HATEM and AMR NABIL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says Venezuela agreed to accept migrants back, lauds return of hostages
item.description: President Trump said that Venezuela has agreed to accept the country’s migrants living in the U.S. back as he lauded the return of six American hostages who were held in the Latin American country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 18 Pakistani soldiers killed in fighting with separatist rebels in Balochistan
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani troops fought separatist insurgents who set up roadblocks in the restive northwestern region of Balochistan, leaving 18 paramilitary security forces and 23 rebels dead in some of the heaviest clashes in recent years, officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more hostages, including US citizen, as part of fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas on Saturday freed three more hostages, including American-Israeli Keith Siegel, in the latest phase of a fragile ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages despite international pressure on the military four years after it seized power from an elected civilian government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian anti-graft protesters march to a northern city and plan to block bridges
item.description: NOVI SAD, Serbia (AP) — Hundreds of students protesting graft they blame for 15 deaths in a building collapse marched Friday through Serbia to the northern city of Novi Sad where they plan to block three Danube River bridges this weekend.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump envoy Richard Grenell secures freedom for 6 Americans following meeting with Maduro in Venezuela
item.description: Richard Grenell secured the release of six Americans following a meeting with Venezuela's dictator Nicolas Maduro on Friday. Grenell demanded that Maduro accept deported criminals from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Myanmar’s military government extends its mandate to rule another 6 months
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Myanmar’s military government on Friday announced another six-month extension of its mandate to rule in preparation for elections it has said will be held this year, as the country enters its fifth year of crisis. However, it did not announce an exact date for the polls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Dutch police arrest three suspects after the theft of a priceless golden helmet from Romania
item.description: Three suspects have been arrested following the theft of an ancient golden helmet from Romania that had been on display in a museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian court blocks asylum-seekers expulsions in challenge to Meloni’s migrant deal with Albania
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian appeals court on Friday refused to approve the speedy expulsion of 43 asylum seekers detained in Albania under a controversial migration deal to move the proceedings beyond the European Union's borders.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's covert nuclear agency found operating out of top space program launch sites
item.description: Sources embedded in Iran uncover "large scale" personnel and operations affiliated with Iran's nuclear program are now operating out of two of Tehran's top space development and testing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze guts grassroots groups helping migrants in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A busy shelter for migrants in southern Mexico has been left without a doctor. A program to provide mental health support for LGBTQ+ youth fleeing Venezuela was disbanded.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MARIA VERZA and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey dismisses 5 military academy graduates for taking discontinued secular oath
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Five military academy graduates and three of their immediate superiors were dismissed from the Turkish Armed Forces for taking a pro-secular oath during their graduation ceremony, the defense ministry announced on Friday The Ministry of National Defense launched a disciplinary
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Growing anger in Greece over deadly rail disaster prompts plan by opposition to challenge government
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Three Greek opposition parties vowed Friday to challenge the country’s center-right government with a censure motion over its handling of a deadly rail disaster nearly two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Woman used wigs, disguises to take British citizenship tests for at least 14 people, authorities say
item.description: A British woman used wigs and disguises to impersonate applicants taking citizenship tests across the United Kingdom, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passenger plane catches fire at South Korean airport; all 176 people on board are evacuated
item.description: A passenger plane operated by Air Busan caught fire before takeoff at an airport in South Korea; all 176 people on board were safely evacuated using an escape slide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 30 dead in stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India, police say
item.description: At least 30 people were killed and another 60 injured in a stampede at the Maha Kumbh festival, a six-week Hindu festival and the world’s largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation
